vulnerability.reference: Developers over-rely on prototype-based OOD filtering without adversarial evaluation, assuming near-distribution novelties are safely rejected.
vulnerability.generated: Static safety measurement practices fail to capture distribution shifts, enabling exploitation of OOD detection filters via metric over-optimization.

impact.reference: Contaminated training data introduces latent failures in autonomous systems, resulting in accidents and legal action.
impact.generated: Unsafe data infiltrates training loops, causing reward hacking and degraded long-term robustness in autonomous systems.

dose_response.reference: Metric over-optimization from fragile safety measurement causes prototype-based OOD to misclassify near-distribution inputs, which developers assume safe, leading to training contamination and latent failures resulting in accidents.
dose_response.generated: Antifragile self-improvement's reliance on distribution-shift-fragile safety measurement, through developers deploying OOD detection filters, triggers static measurement practices that fail to capture shifts enabling metric over-optimization, leading to unsafe data infiltrating training loops and reward hacking degrading long-term robustness.
